name: First Commerce
description_short: Community-focused FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, money market accounts, CDs, credit cards, and commercial lending services.
description_long: First Commerce Bank is a traditional community bank operating under First Commerce Bancorp, Inc., with a physical branch network including locations in Teaneck, Jackson, and Lakewood. The bank serves both personal and business customers with a full suite of deposit products and lending services. Their product offerings span personal checking accounts (Free Checking and Ultimate Rewards Checking), savings vehicles (Premier Savings, Relationship Savings), money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with rates as high as 3.50% APY for 7-month terms. For business customers, they provide commercial checking, business savings, treasury management tools (including their ZSuites platform for escrow and subaccounting), ACH origination services, sweep accounts, merchant services, and commercial lending including mortgages and construction loans. They also serve specialized markets including non-profits (with dedicated non-profit checking) and municipal/government entities. First Commerce Bank distinguishes itself through community-focused banking, competitive deposit rates, multi-language support (English, Hebrew, Spanish), and industry-specific solutions like IOLTA accounts for legal professionals and ZSuites for complex business treasury needs. The bank maintains FDIC insurance backing and pays dividends to shareholders, indicating stable operations. However, as a regional bank with limited branch locations, First Commerce may not suit customers seeking nationwide convenience or digital-first banking experiences comparable to large national banks or neobanks.
services: Personal checking accounts (Free Checking, Ultimate Rewards Checking)
Personal savings accounts (Premier Savings, Relationship Savings)
Personal money market accounts (Relationship Money Market, Premier Money Market)
Certificates of Deposit (7-month at 3.50% APY, 24-month at 3.25% APY)
Personal credit cards (Everyday Rewards+ Card, Travel Rewards+ Card)
Business checking accounts (Free Business Checking, Premier Business Checking)
Business money market accounts (Business Relationship, Premier Business Money Market)
Commercial and small business lending (mortgages, construction loans)
Business credit cards (Business Cash Preferred, Smart Business Rewards)
Sweep accounts for cash flow management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Competitive CD rates up to 3.50% APY for 7-month terms
Ultimate Rewards Checking offers higher interest rates, fee waivers, and rate bumps
Specialized products for niche markets: IOLTA accounts, non-profit checking, municipal banking
ZSuites treasury management platform provides modern escrow and subaccounting tools
VIP Perks program with exclusive benefits for customers maintaining $250,000+ combined balances
Digital banking platform for online access to accounts and services
cons: Limited branch network restricts physical access compared to national banks
Website content does not specify APR rates for credit cards or lending products
No mention of mobile app features or mobile-specific banking capabilities
Unclear fee structures for various accounts and services from available website information
Limited information about minimum balance requirements for most deposit accounts
best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs seeking specialized treasury management and merchant services
Non-profit organizations needing dedicated checking and banking support
Community-oriented individuals preferring regional banking relationships over large national chains
Legal professionals and firms utilizing IOLTA accounts for client funds management
diagnosis: First Commerce Bank is best for business owners, non-profits, and community-focused individuals seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates and specialized services like ZSuites treasury management. Primary caveat: limited branch locations and lack of detailed fee/APR information may require direct customer contact to fully understand pricing structures before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
